description_long: This object is a vintage television set with a wooden rectangular cabinet and four slender black legs, giving it a mid-century modern appearance. The front features a large, convex glass screen surrounded by a silver bezel, with a control panel on the right side that includes several round knobs and buttons, as well as a speaker grille. The sides and top are finished in a warm, polished wood veneer, while the back is a solid black panel with ventilation slots. The television's design is boxy and robust, typical of models from the 1950s to 1970s. The overall build suggests a heavy, solid construction, likely containing internal components such as cathode ray tubes and electronic circuitry. This type of television would commonly be found in living rooms, garages, or even on sidewalks awaiting disposal in urban environments.
description: A vintage, mid-century television with a wood cabinet, large convex glass screen, and a side control panel with knobs and buttons.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large convex screen, silver bezel, and right-side control panel with knobs and speaker grille.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the plain wooden side panel and the depth of the cabinet.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing the solid black back panel with ventilation slots and the four supporting legs.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the plain wooden side panel, mirroring the left side.
attribute_bezel_design: thick silver bezel, metallic finish, rectangular, classic mid-century style
attribute_control_button_layout: vertical row of round knobs and buttons on the front right side
